characters.name: Nick Carraway
characters.description: Narrator from Minnesota, Yale graduate, bond salesman who rents a small house in West Egg next to Gatsby's mansion. Second cousin once removed to Daisy.

characters.name: Jay Gatsby
characters.description: Nick's mysterious wealthy neighbor who throws lavish parties at his mansion, associated with the green light across the bay. Background and source of wealth are unknown.

characters.name: Tom Buchanan
characters.description: Nick's college acquaintance, Daisy's husband, old-money wealthy, physically imposing former football player with racist views.

characters.name: Daisy Buchanan
characters.description: Nick's second cousin once removed, Tom's wife, charming and seemingly shallow, lives in East Egg.

characters.name: Jordan Baker
characters.description: Professional golfer, Daisy's friend, somewhat dishonest, becomes Nick's romantic interest.

characters.name: Myrtle Wilson
characters.description: Tom's mistress, lives in the valley of ashes, married to George Wilson the garage owner.

characters.name: George Wilson
characters.description: Garage owner in the valley of ashes, Myrtle's husband, spiritless and anaemic.

characters.name: Meyer Wolfsheim
characters.description: Gatsby's business associate, mentioned in connection with Gatsby's mysterious background.

characters.name: Owl Eyes
characters.description: Drunk man in Gatsby's library who is amazed that the books are real.

voice_profile.tone: lyrical and elegiac with rich metaphorical prose

settings.name: West Egg
settings.description: Nick's small cottage and Gatsby's enormous mansion on the less fashionable side of Long Island.

settings.name: East Egg
settings.description: The Buchanans' house, the fashionable old-money side of Long Island.

settings.name: Valley of Ashes
settings.description: Desolate industrial wasteland between Long Island and New York, site of Wilson's garage and the billboard of the eyes of Doctor T.J. Eckleburg.

settings.name: New York City
settings.description: Tom's apartment where he conducts his affair with Myrtle, site of the drunken party in Chapter II.

settings.name: Gatsby's mansion/parties
settings.description: Gatsby's enormous house where he throws lavish parties every weekend, attended by hundreds of strangers.

plot_threads.thread: Gatsby's mysterious background and source of wealth — rumors and speculation
The green light across the bay — Gatsby watching Daisy's dock, symbolizing his longing
Tom's affair with Myrtle Wilson and the tension it creates
Nick's growing entanglement with the world of East Egg and West Egg
Gatsby and Daisy's past connection, hinted at by Jordan Baker

world_rules: Jazz Age class divisions: old money (East Egg) versus new money (West Egg)
Prohibition-era hypocrisy — alcohol flows freely at Gatsby's parties despite the law
The American Dream mythology — self-invention and the pursuit of wealth and status
